judgment.summary: Deere & Company presents a mixed fundamental picture with strong profitability and analyst confidence offset by recent revenue declines and a highly leveraged balance sheet.
judgment.earningsRecap: The last reported quarter (ending 2026-01-31) saw EPS of $2.42, beating the estimate of $2.10 by 15.4%. The next earnings date is confirmed for 2026-05-21.
positives: Profit margin of 10.3% and operating margin of 17.5% indicate strong operational efficiency.
Return on equity at 19.6% demonstrates effective use of shareholder capital.
The forward P/E of 24.25x is lower than the trailing P/E of 31.49x, suggesting potential for future earnings growth.
A consensus 'buy' rating from 23 analysts and a mean target price of $665.10 suggest positive external sentiment.
The company beat its last EPS estimate by a significant 15.4%.
negatives: Revenue growth of -11.1% and earnings growth of -8.5% year-over-year indicate a recent downturn in top-line and bottom-line performance.
The Debt/Equity ratio of 376.02x is exceptionally high, signaling substantial financial leverage and risk.
Despite strong margins, the Price/Book ratio of 5.73x suggests the company is trading at a premium to its book value.
The PEG ratio of 1.70x, while not extreme, implies that the stock price may be ahead of its expected earnings growth rate.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter at -14.2% suggest continued top-line pressure in the near term.

news.paragraph: Deere & Company is approaching its second-quarter earnings report, with analysts closely watching key metrics and providing target price insights, while the company also faces a new right-to-repair lawsuit concerning its construction equipment.  The broader market sentiment is mixed due to oil price movements and anticipation of other major earnings.
